sections.paragraph:
not used in Kal; prop. to soothe, to stroke; Arabic سبح to swim, which is done by stroking the water; hence-

Piel-

(1) to still the waves, Psa 89:10 (mulcere fluctus, Virg. Æn. i. 70); anger, Pro 29:11. Compare חִלָּה.

(2) to praise, pr. to soothe with praises (Arabic سَبَّحَ, Eth. ሰብሐ፡ id.), Ecc 8:15 especially God, Psa 63:4 117:1 147:12 .

(3) to pronounce happy, Ecc 4:2 (where שַׁבֵּחַ stands for מְשַׁבֵּחַ). Compare Chald.

Hiphil, i.q. Piel No. 1, to still (waves), Psa 65:8.

Hithpael, followed by בְּ to boast in anything, Psa 106:47 1Ch 16:35.
